messages.content:
Which of the following are valid partitioning types?
(choose two)
MBR
GPT
BIOS
PC
MBR and GPT are valid partitioning types.

Which of the following commands will check hard disk GPT partitions?
(choose three)
cgdisk
gfdisk
sgdisk
gdisk
sfdisk
cgdisk, sgdisk, and gdisk will check hard disk GPT partitions.

The process (ps) command shows only processes running in the current shell by default.
True or False?
True
False
False. The `ps` command shows all processes running on the system, not just those in the current shell.

The free command outputs statistics about:
CPU usage
Memory usage
Disk usage
Software usage
The `free` command outputs statistics about memory usage.

The free command outputs statistics about:
CPU usage
Memory usage
Disk usage
Software usage
The `free` command outputs statistics about memory usage.

To make changes permanent for kernel parameter files found under /proc/sys, the following file can have entries added to it:
/etc/procsys.conf
/etc/sysctl.conf
/etc/procctl.conf
/etc/sysinfo.conf
To make changes permanent for kernel parameter files found under `/proc/sys`, the `/etc/sysctl.conf` file can have entries added to it.

Which of the following commands will display the IP address on a Linux system?
ifconfig
dig
route
ipconfig
`ifconfig` command will display the IP address on a Linux system.

The RSA key fingerprint allows the dig command to connect to remote systems.
True or False?
True
False

False. The RSA key fingerprint has nothing to do with the `dig` command. The RSA key fingerprint is a unique identifier for an SSH server, whereas `dig` is a command-line utility for querying DNS (Domain Name System) servers.

Which of the following commands will display the groups that the user bob belongs to?
groups -a
group bob
id bob
all bob
`id bob` will display the groups that the user `bob` belongs to.

Traditional UNIX systems allowed users to belong to how many groups?
16
10,000
1
256
65,000
Traditional UNIX systems allowed users to belong to a maximum of 16 groups.

A value of 0 in the “minimum” password aging field means the user cannot change their password.
True or False?
True
False
False. A value of 0 in the "minimum" password aging field means the user can change their password without restriction (i.e., immediately), not that they cannot change their password.

Sudo privileges allow users to execute commands as another user.
True or False?
True
False
True. Sudo privileges allow users to execute commands as another user, typically the root user.
